---
title: "between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_aa — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-12
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [26, 20, 17, 17, 20]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![Three-panel bar plot (A-site, P-site, E-site, left-to-right) showing per-amino-acid log2 fold-change of day_10-vs-day_0 median per-replicate frequency with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint. Bars sorted by log2_FC high-to-low within each panel; blue = enriched at day_10 (log2FC >= 0), red = depleted at day_10 (log2FC < 0). Y-axis range ~-0.65 to +0.6.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/composite/APE_aa_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot_composite.png)

Each panel ranks the 20 amino acids by signed log2_FC (day_10 median / day_0 median) at one ribosome site. Bars above zero are day_10-enriched relative to day_0; bars below zero are day_10-depleted. No significance asterisks are drawn — every bar sits at or above the n=4-vs-n=4 MW exact p-floor (raw p = 0.0286).

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_codon.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_aa.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_codon.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum (two-sided)
- **Design:** n=4 day_10 (BWM_d10_rep2/3 + control_d10_rep2/3) vs n=4 day_0 (BWM_d0_rep2/3 + control_d0_rep2/3); BWM and control reps are pooled inside each timepoint; two-sided MW on per-replicate AA frequencies; BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site = own family of 20 amino-acid hypotheses); effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median_d10 / median_d0).

### Family-level caveats

- **mw-floor-blocking** — MW two-sided p-floor for n=4 vs n=4 is exactly 0.02857; with per-site BH-FDR families of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon), clearing FDR<0.05 is mathematically impossible unless ~every test ties at the floor. Empirically: 0 hits at FDR<0.05 in any of the 6 files.
- **condition-pooled-confound** — n=4 per timepoint is built by pooling 2 BWM + 2 control reps; if BWM and control diverge in their time response, pooling masks divergent signals. All 6 members share this design.
- **n=4-low-power** — n=4 vs n=4 MW has very low power; null results across all 6 files are weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline** — Headlines must explicitly state min raw p ~= 0.0286 and explain why "no FDR hits" here is uninformative, not a biological negative.

---

## Headline

No statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 (0/60) for AA-level day_10 vs day_0 MW with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint (n=4 per side). Min raw p = 0.0286 = the exact MW two-sided floor for n=4 vs n=4 — so "no FDR hits" is structural per the locked `mw-floor-blocking` caveat, not a biological negative. One row at the floor and also the file's only sub-0.05 raw-p row (P-site C, +0.405, p_adj=0.571, flagged `nominal-only, floor`), plus three near-floor cells tied at raw p = 0.0571 (P-site E +0.373, A-site Q -0.406, A-site L -0.368, all p_adj=0.571). Sites P and A both reach the same per-site BH wall (min p_adj = 0.571); E-site is flat (min p_adj = 0.984). Treat as exploratory leads only; the only test in this family that can resolve a real day_10 vs day_0 effect is the per-timepoint Fisher.

## Top hits

Effect column is `log2_FC` (day_10 / day_0 median ratio). `p_value` is the raw Mann-Whitney p; `p_adj` is BH-corrected per A/P/E site (family of 20 amino-acid tests). Each site group is split into one sub-table per sign of effect: positive `log2_FC` means day_10-enriched; negative means day_10-depleted. Within each sub-table, rows are up to 5 per direction ranked by raw `p_value` ascending, with `|log2_FC|` descending as the tiebreaker.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| amino acid       | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---              | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| G (glycine)      | +0.234             | 0.2000            | 0.800                |      |
| I (isoleucine)   | +0.233             | 0.2000            | 0.800                |      |
| R (arginine)     | +0.235             | 0.3429            | 0.810                |      |
| D (aspartate)    | +0.171             | 0.3429            | 0.810                |      |
| F (phenylalanine)| +0.109             | 0.3429            | 0.810                |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid       | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---              | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| Q (glutamine)    | -0.406             | 0.0571            | 0.571                |      |
| L (leucine)      | -0.368             | 0.0571            | 0.571                |      |
| Y (tyrosine)     | -0.401             | 0.2000            | 0.800                |      |
| W (tryptophan)   | -0.496             | 0.4857            | 0.810                |      |
| S (serine)       | -0.165             | 0.4857            | 0.810                |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| amino acid       | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag                |
| ---              | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---                 |
| C (cysteine)     | +0.405             | 0.0286            | 0.571                | nominal-only, floor |
| E (glutamate)    | +0.373             | 0.0571            | 0.571                |                     |
| G (glycine)      | +0.124             | 0.1143            | 0.571                |                     |
| D (aspartate)    | +0.144             | 0.4857            | 0.932                |                     |
| V (valine)       | +0.110             | 0.6857            | 0.932                |                     |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid       | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---              | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| K (lysine)       | -0.271             | 0.1143            | 0.571                |      |
| W (tryptophan)   | -0.620             | 0.2000            | 0.667                |      |
| I (isoleucine)   | -0.159             | 0.2000            | 0.667                |      |
| M (methionine)   | -0.511             | 0.3429            | 0.857                |      |
| H (histidine)    | -0.247             | 0.3429            | 0.857                |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| amino acid       | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---              | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| C (cysteine)     | +0.241             | 0.1143            | 0.984                |      |
| S (serine)       | +0.179             | 0.4857            | 0.984                |      |
| L (leucine)      | +0.116             | 0.4857            | 0.984                |      |
| T (threonine)    | +0.100             | 0.4857            | 0.984                |      |
| Q (glutamine)    | +0.369             | 0.6857            | 0.984                |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid       | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---              | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| M (methionine)   | -0.384             | 0.2000            | 0.984                |      |
| P (proline)      | -0.253             | 0.2000            | 0.984                |      |
| K (lysine)       | -0.128             | 0.6857            | 0.984                |      |
| I (isoleucine)   | -0.047             | 0.6857            | 0.984                |      |
| D (aspartate)    | -0.108             | 0.8857            | 0.984                |      |

### Flag glossary

- `floor` — the row sits at the test's discrete minimum possible raw p-value, given the sample size. For MW two-sided at n=4 vs n=4 this floor is exactly 0.02857. BH-correction over a 20-test per-site family turns one floor hit into `p_adj` ~= 0.571; FDR<0.05 cannot be reached unless nearly every test in the site's family ties at the floor (mathematically possible but biologically implausible).
- `nominal-only` — raw p < 0.05 but `p_adj` >= 0.05. The row would have been flagged significant if tested in isolation, but did not survive BH-FDR correction across its grouping family.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 60
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 0
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 0
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.5714 (P-site C floor; ties with P-K, A-L, A-Q at the same p_adj wall)
- `min raw-p`: 0.02857 (= MW exact floor for n=4 vs n=4); 1 row at the floor (P-site C, +0.405)
- `nominal-only count`: 1 amino acid (P-site C) with raw p < 0.05 across all 60 tests; that same row is also at the n=4-vs-n=4 exact-MW floor, so it carries both `nominal-only` and `floor`
- `p_floor`: 0.02857 (n=4 vs n=4 two-sided exact)
- Per-site BH families:
  - A site: 20 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.571
  - E site: 20 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.984
  - P site: 20 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.571

## Biological interpretation

**The file shows no significant differences at FDR<0.05 across all 60 AA-level day_10-vs-day_0 comparisons, and the no-signal result is structural rather than biological (concrete).** Min raw p across the file is exactly 0.02857 — the MW two-sided exact p-floor at n=4 vs n=4. Even a single floor hit BH-corrects to `p_adj` = 0.571 in a 20-AA per-site family; clearing FDR<0.05 would require nearly every AA at one site to tie at the floor simultaneously. The observed picture (one floor row, one near-floor row, both at the P-site) is the design's ceiling, not evidence of biological equivalence between day_10 and day_0. This file alone cannot adjudicate the day_10-vs-day_0 question; the within-condition Fisher contrasts and per-timepoint Fisher are the tests that can resolve it.

**P-site holds the file's only sub-0.05 raw-p row (P-site C, +0.405, flagged `nominal-only, floor`), with tryptophan-down (-0.620) as the other strongest exploratory cell (exploratory).** Both P and A sites reach the same per-site BH wall at min `p_adj` = 0.571, and E-site is flat (min `p_adj` = 0.984); whatever modest day_10-vs-day_0 modulation exists in the pooled-condition picture concentrates at P and A, not at E. P-site C is enriched at day_10 (+0.405, the file's unique sub-0.05 raw-p row, flagged both `floor` and `nominal-only`), P-site E at +0.373 (near-floor, raw p = 0.0571 — just above the nominal threshold), and P-site W is the largest day_10 depletion in the file (-0.620). A cysteine increase and tryptophan decrease at the P site has no obvious mechanistic anchor in the report's domain — both are rare amino acids at stall sites and the `weighted_log2_enrichment-absent` caveat applies acutely to W (median freq < 1%, so the -0.62 magnitude is dominated by a small number of reads). The cell to carry forward most carefully is P-C; the W magnitude is more noise-prone.

**A-site shows a paired closest-to-significant pair (L and Q both day_10-depleted at the BH wall) but no concrete signal (exploratory).** A-site L (-0.368) and A-site Q (-0.406) both sit at raw p = 0.0571 — just above the nominal threshold — with the same `p_adj` = 0.571 as the P-site floor cell (C) and its near-floor neighbour (E). With BWM and control reps pooled in each timepoint, this is a signature that any future day-axis cross-check should look for separately in each condition — a paired day_10-depletion of A-site leucine + glutamine could be biologically consistent (both moderate-frequency residues; no obvious shared mechanism) but cannot be elevated above noise from this file. E-site is essentially flat (min `p_adj` = 0.984).

**What to carry forward.** Concrete claim to quote: this file shows no signal at FDR<0.05 for day_10-vs-day_0 at AA resolution with conditions pooled, and the no-signal result is structural (n=4-floor blocking) rather than biological. Exploratory patterns worth checking in sister tests: (1) does the P-site C day_10 enrichment (the file's unique sub-0.05 raw-p row) reappear at day_10 in `per_timepoint_fisher_aa.csv` (which holds condition rather than timepoint), and does the codon-resolution sister `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_codon.csv` localize the signal to TGT vs TGC? (2) does the P-site W day_10 depletion reproduce in the codon sister at TGG, or is its magnitude a `weighted_log2_enrichment-absent` artifact at a rare-AA cell? (3) does the within-condition Fisher `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv` show a P-site driven d10-vs-d0 pattern in either BWM or control individually? (4) does the **paired A-site day_10-depletion of Q and L** (both at raw p = 0.0571, |effect| ~= 0.4, same `p_adj` = 0.571 as the P-site cells) reproduce in the codon sister at the Gln (CAA/CAG) and Leu (CTN/TTA/TTG) codon families, or at A-site in either of the within-condition Fishers? Noise to ignore: every E-site cell (uniformly `p_adj` ~= 0.98); A-site cells outside the Q/L pair with raw p >= 0.2 (Y, W, S and all A-Enriched rows) are the file's low-signal background and should not be quoted as exploratory leads.

## Methods

Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum two-sided on per-replicate frequencies, n=4 day_10 (BWM_day10_rep2/3 + control_day10_rep2/3) vs n=4 day_0 (BWM_day0_rep2/3 + control_day0_rep2/3), BH-FDR per site (each A/P/E site = 20-AA family). Effect column is `log2_FC` of medians; test statistic is `U_stat`. The test answers "do day_10 and day_0 reps differ in per-rep AA frequency at this site?" — pooling BWM and control reps within timepoint makes condition-by-time interactions invisible. Does *not* answer "does BWM behave differently across time" (that is `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0`).

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **mw-floor-blocking.** Raw-p is clamped at 0.0286 (the n=4 vs n=4 MW exact floor); one floor hit BH-corrects to 0.571 in a 20-test family, so "no FDR hits" here is the design's structural ceiling, not biological evidence — do not read the 0/60 result as supporting day_10 ~= day_0 anywhere in the file.
- **condition-pooled-confound.** Each timepoint's n=4 is 2 BWM + 2 control; a BWM-specific time response and a control-specific time response that point in opposite directions cancel inside the same MW — when a cell here is null, check `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_aa` before concluding "no day-axis change."
- **n=4-low-power.** Modest-to-low power at n=4 vs n=4; null is weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline.** The headline above explicitly flags 0.0286 as the achievable raw-p minimum and explains why the FDR null is structural; read the headline as "0/60 by design," not as a biological negative.
- **weighted_log2_enrichment-absent.** `log2_FC` is the median ratio and gives equal column real estate to rare AAs (W ~0.5%, C ~1.5%) and common AAs (K, E ~10%); the largest |effect| in the file is P-site W (-0.62) at a rare-AA cell — magnitude rankings should weight common-AA cells more heavily.
- **small-bh-family-discreteness.** The observed minimum `p_adj` of 0.571 is exactly 20 x 0.0286 / 1, the BH wall for a single floor hit in a 20-test family; this confirms the structural ceiling empirically and means the only way `p_adj` would have dropped below 0.5 here is multiple co-located floor hits at the same site (which the file does not show).

### Considered and ruled out

- **asymptotic-with-ties.** Concern that `scipy.stats.mannwhitneyu` might fall back to the asymptotic Z approximation under tied per-rep frequencies (more likely than at n=6 vs n=6 because the per-arm n is smaller), which would shift p-values away from the exact-floor bound. **Ruled out, empirically verified** (audit script: `scripts/_for_claude_mw_branch_audit.py --design between_timepoint --timepoints day10 day0 --level aa`): **0 of 60** (site, AA) tests have any rank ties in the pooled 8-element sample, and `scipy.stats.mannwhitneyu(method='auto')` chose the **exact** branch for every single test. Recomputed p-values match the pipeline CSV to ~1e-16, so the pipeline's reported p-values are the right scipy auto-branch choice (60/60 exact). As a counterfactual, forcing `method='asymptotic'` for all 60 tests shifts raw p-values by at most 0.0305 (median 0.0061), flips **zero** raw-p<0.05 decisions, and leaves the per-site BH conclusion unchanged: 0 hits at FDR<0.05 either way (min p_adj 0.61 / 0.56 / 0.98 at A/P/E under asymptotic vs the as-shipped 0.571 / 0.571 / 0.984). The branch choice does not affect any FDR-level conclusion in this file.

*(No further per-CSV caveats apply here; the locked family caveats already cover the structural failure mode, and the per-CSV column-shape note plus BH-discreteness note are sufficient.)*

---

## Plots — individual

![A-site day_10-vs-day_0 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (N, R, G, I, D, F, M, E, V, T, K, P, H, C, S, A, L, Y, Q, W). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-0.6 to +0.6. Largest enrichment at N (~+0.55); largest depletions at W, Q, Y (~-0.5 to -0.4).](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/individual/site_A_aa_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot.png)

![P-site day_10-vs-day_0 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (C, E, D, G, V, R, N, A, Y, S, P, F, L, T, I, Q, H, K, M, W). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-0.65 to +0.6. Largest enrichment at C (~+0.40, the file's floor row); largest depletions at W (~-0.62) and M (~-0.51).](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/individual/site_P_aa_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot.png)

![E-site day_10-vs-day_0 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (Q, C, S, G, L, T, N, E, F, Y, R, V, A, H, I, W, D, K, P, M). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-0.6 to +0.6. Largest enrichment at Q (~+0.37); largest depletion at M (~-0.38).](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/individual/site_E_aa_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot.png)
